action: Moves quietly beside Jade at the fire, preparing the fish with practiced hands.
thought: She reached me and I didn't pull away.

action: Tends the fire and cooks the fish, moving between them with renewed energy.
thought: Did she just look at him like that?

action: Moves close to the fire, watching Marcus work, her eyes bright with something she can't quite name.
thought: He let me stay beside him. That's everything.

action: Sits alone at the shore, watching the three of them work and laugh by the fire, her hand on her belly.
thought: They have each other. I have no one.

action: Moves between the fire and Judy's side, the pup's eyes sad, finally settling beside her in the dark.
thought: She's alone. But my warmth helps. I stay.

description: Evening finds them cooking fresh fish at the fire—Marcus and Jade glow beside the flames while Ryan tends it, and Judy watches from the shore with the stray at her side.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We actually pulled it off. Fresh fish—real food.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better than the stores.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So much better. The way you showed us... I felt it click.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's got a gift for it. Something in his hands just knows the water.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You were quick to learn.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because you taught me. Really taught me. Not just the how—the why.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay, now I'm envious. What'd he teach you that he didn't teach me?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Different things. You think in structures. You'd build something to catch them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's right. You would. But Marcus—he reads the water like it's talking to him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That so? That's—that's something.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Everyone has their way. Building's yours. Reading's mine.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And mine?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You just... follow the feeling. It works.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It does. Worked today anyway. You two were quiet out there at the pools, yeah?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He was teaching me. Showing me things I never would have seen.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She asked good questions.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Jade's good at that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The stray came with us too. Even the pup wanted to learn.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And Judy?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She stayed at the shore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: She was tired. The pregnancy—it's wearing her down.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. She's been struggling.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should make sure she eats well tonight. Make sure she knows she belongs.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Already thinking about it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We have enough now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: For the first time in days. Thank you for this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Let's eat before it gets cold.

action: Retreats to his tent without sitting by the fire, afraid of what he's letting himself feel.
thought: I let her too close too fast.

action: Sits by the dying fire with Jade, watching Marcus pull away and sensing something has shifted between them.
thought: Something changed. I felt it today.

action: Sits by the fire beside Ryan, frustrated and confused as Marcus withdraws, trying to understand what scared him.
thought: He was open. Why is he running?

action: Stands on the dark beach, watching the firelit group from a distance, the stray her only companion.
thought: They have each other. I'm still alone.

action: Presses close to Judy on the beach, choosing her over the fire group, whimpering softly as she watches the distant flames.
thought: She's hurting. I stay.

description: At the dying fire, Marcus suddenly retreats to his tent in fear, leaving Jade reaching after him—while Ryan watches the fracture and Judy grieves alone on the beach with the stray.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You were different today. With Marcus at the tide pools.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I felt different. Like I could actually reach him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He doesn't usually let people in like that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. He was just... there. Fully there with me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Does he know what that meant to you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't think he's thinking that far ahead. He's just scared.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire's burning down.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I've got it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's late.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You don't have to leave.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm tired.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You weren't tired ten minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That was different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's just the fire getting low. We're all exhausted.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why are you running from this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not running. I'm going to sleep.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is there fish left?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Plenty. Come sit with us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't want to... intrude. On whatever this is.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not intruding.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The stray's the only one who actually chooses to be with me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's not true.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tomorrow. Talk tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't just shut down every time you feel something.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not shutting down. I'm protecting—

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Protecting what, Marcus?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Us. He's protecting us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Or himself.

action: He walks to the cliff base in the grey dawn light, needing space from what he let happen.
thought: She saw me run. Can I face her?

action: He's up early tending the fire, laying out water bowls, watching the east sky lighten.
thought: Something shifted last night. I felt it.

action: She wakes restless and walks toward the cliffs, drawn by something she can't name.
thought: He was there. Where did he go?

action: She sits at the beach's edge with the pup, watching the water, feeling the distance from last night.
thought: They have each other. I'm still alone.

action: The pup presses warm against Judy's leg, sensing her sadness and staying close.
thought: She's sad. I stay. I'm here.

description: Jade finds Marcus alone at the cliff base at dawn, and they face what shattered between them last night.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Didn't expect to see you this early.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Didn't expect you to run.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I had to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: From me?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. From how fast it was happening.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We were just cooking fish, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. But you reached for me and I let you. That's when I got scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Of wanting it back.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not a bad thing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For me it is. I lose things.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then stop running.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Can't promise that yet.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Just promise you'll come back.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's enough.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm sorry about last night.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You don't have to apologize for being scared.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What about leaving?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That I didn't like. But I understand.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm still figuring this out.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So am I.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We could figure it out slow.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That sounds nice.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Thank you for coming to find me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Where else would I go?

action: He drifted back from the cliff as the sun climbed, settling near the house porch by midday, watching the camp breathe.
thought: I let her too close too fast.

action: He kept the fire steady through the morning, feeding it wood at intervals, watching the smoke rise and drift across the camp.
thought: Something's shifting. I can feel it.

action: She noticed Judy missing by midday and walked to the beach, found her watching the waves, took her hand, and pulled her back toward the fire.
thought: I need her to know she belongs.

action: She let Jade find her on the beach, accepted her hand, and walked back toward the fire with her, the stray padding between them both.
thought: Maybe they really do want me.

action: It stayed with Judy through the morning and walked beside her as Jade led them both back, never leaving her side.
thought: She's okay now. I made sure.

description: By midday Jade has found Judy alone at the beach and is pulling her back to camp, the stray padding alongside them, faithful.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I was looking for you. You disappeared.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I just needed to get away from everything for a bit.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Away from me too?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: No. Never from you. But I was watching yesterday and I felt... left behind.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Left behind from what?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: From being part of it. You were fishing with Marcus and Ryan. The three of you were solving something together and I was just standing on the shore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Judy, we wanted you there. We asked.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Did you really? Or were you just being kind because you're kind to everyone?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm kind because I mean it. Sit with me.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm eight months pregnant. I can't fish. I can't swim. I can't even carry water far. What use am I?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're more than what you can do. You know that, right?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't know anything anymore. I just know I'm terrified.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What are you terrified of?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: That I'll lose this. All of it. You, them, the pup. The safety I finally found.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You won't lose us. We chose you. We're still choosing you.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But I'm not choosing myself. I'm just... depending on all of you to keep me here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And we will. That's what we do. We keep each other here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: What if I'm too much? What if carrying this baby is too much to ask of anyone?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we ask anyway. Come back with me. Come back to the fire where you belong.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't know if I can face them. After pulling away like this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: They won't question it. Ryan will just be glad you're there. And Marcus... Marcus understands about needing space.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: He ran last night, didn't he?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He did. And this morning we talked. Sometimes people need to run before they can stay.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is that what this is for me? Running?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Maybe. Or maybe you just needed to be alone for a while. That's okay. But now come back. Come back with me.

action: Moves to the tide pools at low tide and finds an abundance of exposed mussels, gathering with quiet precision.
thought: They needed me. I knew what to do.

action: Arrives at the tide pools to find Marcus already working and joins him, moving carefully beside him in the shallow water.
thought: He's letting me in. We're actually okay.

action: Scouts the coves at low tide and discovers an easy pocket of fresh fish, filling her basket methodically and moving toward the group.
thought: We're stronger together than we ever were.

action: Moves to the fire with quiet purpose, preparing cooking stones and clearing space, finding her role in the afternoon's gathering.
thought: I can do something useful here.

action: Pads after Judy to the firepit and settles close, watching her work with steady presence, protective and calm.
thought: She's safe. I'm here with her.

description: At the tide pools during low tide, Ryan finds Marcus already gathering mussels; they work side by side in the shallow water, and in the quiet, Marcus finally opens up.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You found them. I wasn't sure where to start.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Low tide. They're exposed for an hour.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Can I help?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Move slow. Don't splash.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Like this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Working with you is easier somehow.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: How?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're steady. I'm less anxious around you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not steady.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You are. You just hide it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Maybe.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're good, right?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. We are.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because you meant what you said about staying?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I did. I still do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Everyone could be like this if they stopped being afraid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Jade doesn't fear. And Judy—she reaches out anyway.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You came back from the cliff.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You were here. That mattered.

action: He returns from the tide pools with Ryan as the sun lowers, settling by the fire with an ease that wasn't there before.
thought: I'm different here now. It feels okay.

action: He comes back from gathering mussels with Marcus, moving directly to the firepit as evening deepens, every gesture showing he's exactly where he wants to be.
thought: Marcus finally sees me. Really sees me.

action: She drifts to the firepit as darkness settles, drawn by the light and the people gathering around it.
thought: They're all showing up. Really here together.

action: She settles near the warmth of the fire, the stray curled against her side, finally not flinching at the closeness.
thought: I'm wanted. They keep coming back.

action: The pup presses close to Judy by the fire, her brown fur catching the firelight, protective and finally at rest.
thought: She's safe now. I'm here with her.

description: As evening settles, Ryan and Marcus sit by the fire together, Jade and Judy nearby with the stray; for the first time, there's room to breathe together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You know, today was different. Working the tide with you—it felt right.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. It did.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all I'm getting? Just yeah?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What else is there? You know what I mean.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's saying yes completely. Marcus doesn't waste words on empty things.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I've felt like we were holding our breath around each other. Today we just... didn't.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Wasn't planned. Just happened naturally.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Everything felt different today. I'm not afraid anymore. Not like I was before.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because you're safe now. Truly safe.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: How do you know that though? How can anyone really be sure?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because we chose this. All of us, choosing it together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup chose you too. Won't leave your side.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Animals know the truth before people do. She knows you matter here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: No one's ever needed me like this. Not ever.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well you're needed now. By all of us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You belong here. It's not a question anymore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's just fact now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I want to believe that so badly.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then believe it. We're not going anywhere tomorrow or the day after.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not ever.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. I'm going to believe it then.

action: Sits with the group at the fire as it dies, then rises and heads back to his tent, finally at peace.
thought: I'm okay. They're okay with me.

action: Gathers with everyone at the fire as it dies, then settles on the porch with Judy and the stray for the night.
thought: This is what I wanted. All of us together.

action: Leans into the warmth of the fire and the people around it, then drifts toward her hammock as night deepens.
thought: I'm not floating anymore. I've landed.

action: Sits with the group at the dying fire, the stray warm against her leg, then stays on the porch with Ryan as sleep comes.
thought: They want me here. This is real.

action: Curls tightly against Judy at the fire, then follows her to the porch where her warmth is the safest place on the island.
thought: Home is where she is. Always.

description: Around the dying firepit, four sit in easy silence as night deepens — Marcus letting go, Ryan finally at rest, Jade found, and Judy no longer trembling.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's strange, isn't it? A few days ago we were strangers.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're still kind of strangers. We're just... choosing to know each other now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I was so sure you'd all decide to leave me behind.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We didn't leave you. We went to the tide pools and came back.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And we came back for you. All of us. That matters.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What changed? I mean, why now? Why today?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because nobody pretended anymore. We just... were.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I wanted to believe that. That it was real. I wasn't sure.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It is. Look at Marcus. He's sitting here. By choice.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And he's not counting down the minutes until he can escape.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not. That part's gone now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Are you saying... you want to stay? With us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm saying I'm already here. And that feels like enough now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all any of us needed to hear.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Tomorrow things might be hard again. Someone will be hungry or tired.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But tonight we're all together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's the whole island right there. Tonight.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should sleep. Let the fire rest.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: In the morning we'll figure out shelter, and food, and survival stuff again.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But for now I get to feel safe.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Go to bed. All of you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You first, actually. You need rest like everyone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Will you be okay? In your tent?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I think I will be.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Goodnight, Marcus. And... thank you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Goodnight, all of you.

action: Emerges from his tent as Ryan approaches, moving toward him without the usual hesitation.
thought: He makes this easier than I expected.

action: Greets Marcus at his tent with quiet warmth and proposes fishing at the tide pools together.
thought: He's staying. He's actually choosing this.

action: Stretches by her hammock, watches Ryan and Marcus walk toward the water, offers her help.
thought: They're finally finding their rhythm.

action: Wakes on the porch as the others move into the day, settling the pup against her.
thought: They're all okay. I'm okay too.

action: Follows Ryan and Marcus down the path toward the tide pools, tail raised and curious.
thought: Where they go, I go now.

description: Early morning: Ryan meets Marcus at his tent with quiet warmth and proposes fishing together—Marcus agrees, and the two head down the path in easy conversation.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Morning. You're up early.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Couldn't sleep much longer.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I was thinking we could fish today, if you wanted. The food's running low.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tide pools? You're better at finding the shellfish.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't mind going.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Good. Let me grab the basket and we can head out.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You two are heading out?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fishing. We should be back by noon.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'll put water on for coffee. Some of those dried leaves from yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That sounds good.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Perfect. Keep an eye on the fire for us?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course. Go on.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How are you feeling this morning?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Lighter than before. Like I can actually breathe.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's good. Really good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm still scared sometimes. Still not sure I'm doing this right.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You are. You're here, you're staying. That's everything.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You make it easier. I don't know what you're doing, but it's working.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm just being here. Being honest. You're doing the hard part.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Where should we start?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Over there. I saw movement in that crevice yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll check the shallow shelf. There's usually something good there.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Sounds like a plan. Let's do this.

action: Marcus returns from fishing with Ryan, his walls lower, moving among the group with less distance than before.
thought: He didn't push. Staying is easier than running.

action: Ryan guides everyone toward the catch, steady and warm, drawing all of them into the shared work.
thought: He's choosing to stay. I can see it.

action: Jade watches the change in Marcus with sudden fascination, kneeling to greet the stray while noticing his openness.
thought: Something shifted in him this morning. He's different.

action: Judy steadies on the porch with her hand on her belly, reaching to help as the group gathers.
thought: They want me here. That's real. I'm staying.

action: The stray moves between them all with certainty, pressing closest to Judy as her loyal constant.
thought: She's my safe place. Always.

description: Back from fishing with Ryan, Marcus is visibly warmer and less guarded; Jade watches the shift in him with sudden, knowing fascination.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Three good ones. Marcus spotted them in the pool—he's got a real eye.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The tide was right.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You two look different. Closer, somehow.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's staying, Jade. Really staying this time.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Easier than I thought it would be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I can feel it in you. The change.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm glad. Truly. It makes me feel safer somehow.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: pads closer, tail low but present, pressing soft against Jade's leg

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You feel it too, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I want to cook them right. Help me take these somewhere quiet?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The drying rack is falling apart. We could gather wood, smoke what we catch.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A few days ago you wouldn't have planned that far. You're thinking about us now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Still planning. Just differently. Not just running anymore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What changed, Marcus? Tell me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He didn't push me away when I let the wall down.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You met me halfway. That's all I wanted.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I can help gather wood. Tell me what you need.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: rises, moves to Judy's side, tail steady, eyes on her face

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He knows she's struggling.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He knows what it feels like to be on the edge.

dialogue.line: I can see that. It's really good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.line: We're taking them to the tide pools to clean them properly. You should come.

dialogue.speaker: judy

dialogue.line: No. Not this time.

dialogue.line: I won't.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not. You're here.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I am. And I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Do you? Do you really?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I do, Ryan. I'm here too.

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The camp's empty. We can't just sit and wait.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The tide's low. We can build a trap in the shallows—should catch something by evening.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How long does it take to build?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: A few hours if we work together. The design's simple—just rocks and branches made into a funnel.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Show me. I'll help you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay. We'll need twenty rocks and maybe thirty branches. Not too thick.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Like this one?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's good. We stack them into a circle, then narrow the mouth so the fish swim in but can't swim out.

dialogue.line: You've done this before.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Once. Years ago, before I came here. I was running then, too. I wasn't staying anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And now you are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yes. Because of you.

dialogue.line: What would you need from me?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'll be there. On the rocks, close, watching.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You mean that?

dialogue.speaker: ryan

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We will. I promise.

dialogue.line: You don't have to be alone anymore. You're choosing that now.

thought: I can hold them steady. One day at a time.

action: Gathers everyone by the firepit and organizes the work: he'll scout the stream, Marcus will search for water, Jade will help Judy prepare the cooking pit.
thought: I keep us moving forward. That's my part.

action: Sits with Ryan and Marcus by the fire, speaking her uncertainty—that maybe they're supposed to be different now, that she doesn't know what she wants.
thought: I don't know who I am without structure.

action: Waits by the porch with the stray, listening to the men plan, her anxiety rising—testing whether they'll really stay.
thought: Will they leave? Will I be alone?

action: Stays pressed to Judy's leg, whimpering softly, sensing her fear and refusing to leave her side.
thought: She needs me. I stay with her.

description: They divided labor by the firepit—Ryan and Marcus scouting for water and food, Jade staying with Judy to prep the pit, the stray at her side.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The fire held. We rebuild—the house frame, a lean-to for Marcus, Jade's hammock.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water's the problem. We're bleeding it out and nothing since the rain trough.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Does it matter? We lost everything and we're still here, talking. Maybe that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's enough for now. But Judy needs walls before the baby comes. Four weeks, maybe less.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He's right. And we need water and food today, or the rest doesn't matter anyway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: When I woke up with no hammock, I thought—maybe we're supposed to be different now. No shelter. Just fire and each other.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's beautiful. But you'll be freezing and sick in two days without a roof.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. I'm just... processing. When you lose everything, you have to figure out who you are without it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're still Jade. Still the same person. Just colder and wetter.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not how it feels.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'll scout the stream for fish and water. Marcus, you check inland for a spring or fresh source.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Half day, maybe less. We're back by late afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Jade, can you and Judy prep the firepit for cooking? We bring back whatever we find and eat first, then build.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if you don't find anything out there?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then we make do. But we will find something.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup stays with Judy. So she knows we're coming back.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is she that scared?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yes. And we promised her we wouldn't leave. So we don't.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Any of us.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. I'll keep her steady. Go find water.

action: He returned from scouting with Ryan, water-heavy, and settled by the fire as Jade began to speak her mind.
thought: She needs to choose. I finally understand.

action: He carried branches laden with fruit back to camp, set them down, and listened as Jade voiced her unsettledness.
thought: She felt trapped by our promises.

action: She waited with Judy by the fire all morning, and when the men returned, she finally let out what had been bothering her since dawn.
thought: I can choose to stay. That changes everything.

action: She sat by the fire with the pup, anxious, then grateful, then defensive, then understanding as Jade spoke.
thought: She's not leaving. She's choosing us. Oh god.

action: The pup stayed pressed against Judy's side throughout, sensing her fear and relief, whimpering once when the conversation grew tense.
thought: She is safe. I keep her safe.

description: They gathered at the firepit as Ryan and Marcus returned with water and fruit, but Jade's unsettledness pulled the group into an honest conversation about choice, safety, and staying together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We found a spring about a hundred yards east. And some fruit trees.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You found water? Thank god. How are you both?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fine. The path is clear.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's... really good. We needed that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You okay? You've been quiet since we got back.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. Just thinking about this morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What about it?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The way you both promised Judy you'd stay. It was right, she needed that. But it unsettled me.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Unsettled? Are you... are you leaving?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No. But you decided I was staying before asking me. Like you'd already settled it for all of us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We were talking to Judy, not deciding for you. You were right there.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. But it felt like the choice was made and I just... had to go along.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You want to leave?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No. I want to stay. But I need it to be my choice, not something decided for me.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. I was so scared you'd all disappear. I didn't think about how that felt to you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're safe. You know that, right? That's separate from me choosing to stay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So what would choosing look like? For you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't know yet. I feel wanted, which is... new. But I need to feel like I could leave if I wanted. Even if I don't.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then choose us. Every day.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Every day sounds permanent. Can I just choose today?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Today is enough. What about tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'll decide tomorrow. But for today, I'm choosing you.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Even one day at a time means you'll still be here. Right?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. I'll still be here.

action: Settles by the firepit with Ryan, watching the flames and planning tomorrow.
thought: She keeps choosing. That's what real looks like.

action: Sits beside Marcus, warm and attentive, listening to Jade and Judy unfold by the fire.
thought: They needed to hear each other. We all did.

action: Holds Judy's hand as the evening deepens, their voices quiet but real in the firelight.
thought: I can choose again tomorrow. Just like they said.

action: Lets the stray's weight anchor her as words finally, finally become believable.
thought: I'm choosing to stay. Every day. Maybe I'm safe.

action: Rests against Judy's leg, ears fully relaxed, a small brown anchor in the evening firelight.
thought: She is safe now. I keep her safe.

description: They've moved through the hard talk and settled into something quieter: Marcus and Ryan planning fishing, Jade holding Judy's hand, the stray's calm presence keeping them all anchored.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Those berries won't last. Water's the problem—we need to figure that out.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fish trap. We have the wood.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're planning what's next. Even after all of that?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: After what? What are you thinking?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You promised not to leave, and now you're just moving forward?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It did. Tomorrow we move. That's what it means.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We stay because we choose to. Every day. Jade, it's real.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But will you stay tomorrow? And the next day?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yes. I promised. All of us did.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I keep waiting for it to be a lie. For you to leave and—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're here. Look at the pup.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She's not afraid anymore. That's how you know it's real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The dog knows what we're still learning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: She really does.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I choose to stay—tomorrow and the next day and every day after.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all any of us can do, Judy.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll check the trap in the morning. Food by midday.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How is he always this steady?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because you remind him he doesn't carry it alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How do I do that? Just by being here?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Exactly. By choosing. By staying. He needs to know.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It is real. You're choosing. I see it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm only just learning what real is.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The pup taught me. She doesn't wonder if I'll leave. She just stays.

action: He feeds the fire and settles into the night, watching the flames grow quieter as darkness deepens around him.
thought: She reached back when I finally did.

action: He checks the house one more time, ensuring Judy is resting well and the stray is comfortable beside her.
thought: All of them. All here. That's everything.

action: She brings driftwood to the fire and settles beside Marcus, and as the night deepens they move closer, no longer afraid.
thought: He stopped running and finally let me in.

action: She settles into the house porch with the stray curled against her, finally feeling the weight lift from her chest.
thought: They meant it. They're staying with me.

action: The puppy presses close to Judy through the night, ears alert but body relaxed, knowing the family is safe together.
thought: She is safe. We are safe together.

description: Marcus and Jade finally break their careful distance, sitting close as night settles, honest about what they're beginning to feel.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should talk about it. About how close everything's getting.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Are you scared?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Of what?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of losing it. This. Us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm scared of running.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you want to?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then don't.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: (looks at her) Wasn't planning to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because if you leave, I think... (stops, shakes head) I can't say it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Say it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It would hurt. Really hurt. And I hate needing someone like that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not leaving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can say it, but tomorrow something changes, and everything falls—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tomorrow I'll say it again. And the day after. And the day after that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How are you sure?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not. But I'm staying anyway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: (reaches over, takes his hand) Okay then.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: (doesn't pull away, holds it back) Okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You won't disappear?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. I won't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Good. Good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: (leans slightly closer) We can do this.

action: Woke before dawn and waited by his tent, watching the camp stir, thinking about Jade.
thought: Will she come? Did last night change anything?

action: Checked the water containers at the firepit and found them completely empty.
thought: We need a plan. Food and water today.

action: Moved through the camp and converged on Ryan at the firepit, realizing the day's urgent work.
thought: Where is Marcus? Is he okay?

action: Emerged from the house onto the porch, peaceful and stable, watching the group move through camp.
thought: They kept their promises. I'm safe.

action: Followed Judy out to the porch, pressing against her leg, content in her presence.
thought: She is safe. We are safe.

description: Jade and Ryan face the empty water containers and organize the day's work at the firepit; Marcus waits at his tent for Jade; Judy is stable on the porch with the stray.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The water's completely gone. How did we not refill yesterday?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We were all focused on... talking. That needed to happen.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It did. And I'm not blaming anyone. But now we need water. And food. And urgency.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. Marcus can fish. We just... we figure it out together, right?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the plan. But he's been up since dawn. Didn't want to interrupt whatever's happening between you two.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Whatever we're... I don't even know what we are now. Are we anything?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't have to know yet. But he's going to wait for you to figure it out. That's who he is.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I hate that. I don't want to wait. I want him here. I want both of you here, actually.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You can have that. But first we need to not starve. Then we sort the rest out. Okay?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. Yes. So what's the plan?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I fish today. You and Marcus go gather water from the stream. Judy stays here with the pup, keeps the fire.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if he doesn't want to go? What if he's... I don't know, changed his mind?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He won't have. But if you need reassurance, go ask him. Go now, actually.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Since when are you the one making all the plans? When did that become your thing?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't know. Somewhere between building the house and realizing I need to build things to feel like I matter.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You matter, Ryan. We all know that. You don't have to prove it every day.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe I do, a little. But thank you. Now go. We're losing good daylight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. I'm going. And... thanks for organizing this.

action: Hearing voices at the firepit, he cuts his morning's solitude short and joins the group to help plan the day's forage.
thought: I can't hide anymore. She knows that now.

action: He empties the last of the stores and calls the group together, urgency sharp in his movements and voice.
thought: Everything depends on keeping them steady.

action: She returns from her morning water-gathering to find Ryan and Marcus waiting, and settles between them to work through the plan.
thought: I didn't expect him to show up.

action: She remains on the porch with one hand on the stray's warm flank, watching the three of them work, steady and calm.
thought: They stayed. They all stayed. I'm safe.

action: It curls tight against Judy's leg, sensing the group's focus and energy, its body warm and calm against her thigh.
thought: She is safe. I stay with her.

description: At midday, the empty food and water stores sent Ryan, Jade, and Marcus to the firepit to plan the forage—while Judy kept the stray close on the porch, watching.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The cache is completely empty. No water, no food. We have to move today.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. I already looked this morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: And?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Found a stream bed with water pooling. Not much, but something.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's good. The tide pools north of the beach should have fish.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For traps?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. I can build them, but I need wood and hands.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I can help. I'm not afraid of the water.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. I'm going to need both of you, honestly.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then we should go now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. Yeah. Jade, you know the beach well?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The north part especially. There's a cove that's quieter.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You've been alone there?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: A few times. It felt right, you know?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Can you get us back there?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then we go together.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Wait—what about Judy and the pup? We can't just leave them.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: They're stable. We leave food for the night and come back before sunset.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're confident about that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: When did you start planning ahead like this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Recently.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I was going to suggest the same thing, actually.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know you were.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Do you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Ryan. We're on the same side.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. I'm sorry. The baby, Judy, the pup—it all depends on us.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We won't fail.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Come on. If we're going, we waste no more time.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Stay close. If anyone splits off, we circle back here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're different.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm trying.

action: Returns from the afternoon forage with Ryan and Jade; takes position at the firepit to cook the fish over hot flames, movements deliberate and grounded.
thought: She asked if I'd stay. I meant it.

action: Leads the foraging team back to camp with fish and foraged greens; begins organizing food preparation at the firepit, already thinking about tomorrow's water search.
thought: Everything depends on them knowing I'm here.

action: Returns from the afternoon forage hungry and thirsty; sits at the firepit watching the fish cook, then asks the questions she's been holding since yesterday.
thought: I'm terrified they'll wake up tomorrow and leave.

action: Watches from the house porch as the three return with food, her hand resting on the stray's head, visible relief crossing her face.
thought: They came back together. They're still here.

action: Remains close to Judy on the porch, ears perking up as the smell of cooking fish drifts across the camp, tail beginning to wag with hope.
thought: She is safe. Food is coming.

description: With fish from the afternoon forage sizzling over the firepit, Marcus, Ryan, and Jade work side by side; Jade asks what they're all afraid of, and both men answer her honestly for the first time.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The trap caught three. That's better than yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Greens are wilting. Need to cook soon.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm starving. I can barely feel my feet.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You did good today. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We did what needed doing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did anyone check the south path? I thought I saw more palms there.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should go back tomorrow. The mushrooms there—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water's the priority. Everything else comes after.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fair. But we need variety, or people get sick.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: My throat is killing me. Can we please eat?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. That's why we're here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Found a seepage mark near the boulder watch. Might be a source.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You found that today and didn't say anything?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Didn't want to promise what wasn't sure.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well, let's explore it tomorrow—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can we just eat first?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Right. Sorry.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll tend the fire. Fish needs heat.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: She looks better, doesn't she? Judy.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: She does. Last night helped, I think.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The promises did. She needed to know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you ever worry they'll change? Tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: All the time.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not about staying. I'm sure of that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How are you sure?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because I chose. That's all that matters.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's good. But Jade's right to worry—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not asking for reassurance. I'm asking if you're scared too.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. I wake up every morning afraid something's broken.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It hasn't. Won't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't promise that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. But I can promise I won't run.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The fish is ready when you are, Judy!

action: He sits back from the fire, watching Jade and Ryan speak, his responses spare but honest, telling them what solitude cost him.
thought: Solitude was safer than this. But this is worth it.

action: He sits close to Judy at the fire's edge, taking her hand as the conversation deepens, promising to stay despite his own fear.
thought: I can't leave them, even though I wake afraid.

action: She asks the hardest questions—what scared means, what staying actually means—and listens as both men lay themselves open.
thought: They're actually here. They're choosing this.

action: She sits at the fire with the stray pressed against her leg, listening to the men's confessions, and finally allows herself to believe they meant their promises.
thought: Maybe they really will stay. I can rest.

action: It moves between the group, whimpering softly at tension, pressing its whole body against Judy's leg, sensing her relief as the evening settles.
thought: She is safe now. We are safe together.

description: Jade pushed them to speak their deepest fears tonight—Ryan and Marcus answered with brutal honesty, Judy finally believed them, and the stray pressed close, sensing everything shift.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: When you say you're scared, do you mean it's not real—or it is?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Both. I wake up convinced I dreamed this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's real.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Real but fragile. We're all holding still, afraid to break it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because if it breaks—

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: We're alone again. And I've been alone. I can't go back.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Whimpers, shifts closer to Judy; she rests a hand on its head.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You won't.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You can't promise that. People leave. That's what they do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not people. I'm here. I chose here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: So did I.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But we're terrified. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fear doesn't mean we're leaving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It means we're human. And humans do leave.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: Stands, presses its whole body against Judy's leg; she pulls it close.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not all of them.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You used to be alone by choice.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I was. It felt safe until I realized how much it cost.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: How do you stop being afraid it'll happen again?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: A long pause; Ryan reaches over, takes Judy's free hand.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't. You just stay anyway.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Every day. The way the pup does.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It doesn't even know to be afraid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Neither did we, once.

action: He sits back from the fire, watching the others in the glow, his hand resting on the pup's neck.
thought: I told them everything. They're still here.

action: He stares into the flames, jaw tight, fingers picking at a crack in the wood beside him.
thought: Did I say too much? Will they leave?

action: She wraps her arms around her knees, tilted toward Ryan and Marcus, her gaze flickering between them.
thought: They meant it. So why do I doubt?

action: She settles on the porch steps with the pup at her side, her hand on her belly, breathing slowly.
thought: They promised. I heard them. I believe now.

action: It rests its long body against Judy's leg, one ear twitched toward the fire, content in the quiet.
thought: She is safe. We are safe.

description: Late night by the fire, Ryan breaks the silence with a question clawing at him: the hunger, the thirst, the weight of what they just promised each other.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. I keep thinking about what I said earlier, and now I'm just... what if we can't actually do this?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You mean the promises?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I mean all of it. We're not eating, water's gone, and in three days we're going to be desperate.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We've been desperate since day one.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, but then we weren't depending on each other to stay sane. Now...

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Now if one of us breaks, we all break.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not breaking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You might not have a choice.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: There's always a choice. I chose to stay. I'll choose it again tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But what if I can't? What if the fear just gets worse?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we hold you up. Like you held me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And if you start falling too?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll hold you both.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't carry all of us, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Probably not. But I'm going to try anyway.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the scariest thing you've said all day.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why is it scary?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because if you fail, I'm going to believe it's my fault for needing you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's not how it works.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How does it work, then?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You just show up. You don't owe anyone perfect. Just present.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: When did you become the wise one?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: When you made me admit I'm terrified every morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're all terrified.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. And somehow that makes it better.

action: By dawn he was sitting by his tent when Jade found him, and they talked through the morning light.
thought: I meant every word I told them.

action: He woke early and moved through camp checking the fire and supplies, trying to shake off his anxiety.
thought: Are they really here, or just staying for now?

action: She left her hammock at first light and walked toward the cliff area, drawn to find Marcus waiting.
thought: They meant it. We're actually doing this.

action: She woke to the stray pressed against her side and stayed in bed, letting herself rest for the first time.
thought: They kept their promise. I'm finally safe.

action: The pup woke beside Judy, pressed close as she stirred, completely content in the early morning quiet.
thought: She's here. We are safe now.

description: In early light by the cliff, Marcus sits with last night's weight—until Jade finds him and they speak the quiet truth they both needed to hear.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You didn't sleep.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Couldn't. Kept going over what I said last night.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it. Every word.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. I could hear it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's different in the morning. More real, more terrifying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you're still here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: So are you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: When it gets hard, are you going to run?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm going to try not to. That's all I can promise right now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's all I'm doing too. Just choosing to stay today, and we'll choose again tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then we'll do it together.

action: He walks down from his tent at midday and sits with them at the firepit, despite every instinct telling him to run to the cliff.
thought: I'm terrified and staying anyway.

action: He tends the fire at midday, adding wood and checking the embers, then sits down as Marcus arrives.
thought: Is he really here? Is this real?

action: She drifts over to the fire and settles on the ground, watching Marcus the moment he arrives, eyes warm.
thought: He came down. He's really choosing this.

action: She moves carefully from the house porch toward the firepit, resting most of her weight on a palm trunk, but drawn by the voices.
thought: They're all here. They're not leaving.

action: The pup watches from the edge of the firepit clearing, ears forward, tail uncertain but present, as all four gather together.
thought: They are here. We are safe.

description: At midday, Marcus finally sits with them instead of the cliff — Jade watching, Ryan tending fire, Judy present — and he speaks the truth he's been holding: he's terrified but choosing to stay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I wasn't sure you'd come down.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Neither was I. I almost didn't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you did.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I did.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because of last night?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because of last night. And this morning. And the fact that Jade was right.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: About the choice being real. Being mine. Not just happening to me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So you're choosing this?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm choosing today. Right now. I'm choosing to sit here with you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That matters. More than you know.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Does it matter enough that you'll stay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yes. It does.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The pup's been watching us the whole time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not leaving either.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just like us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because it's safe now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I want it to be. I want it to stay safe. Even when I'm afraid.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You said that. Last night. And this morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I did. And I meant it. I still mean it, even when I want to run.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But you're not running.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. I'm sitting here instead. Terrified. But staying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Staying for what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For you. All of you. For this feeling that maybe I don't have to be alone.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You're not alone.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. I'm starting to know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's enough for today?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: More than enough. For today.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tomorrow I'll choose again.

action: He drifted toward the cliff in early afternoon, doubt creeping back despite his promises, until Jade found him and pulled him back into the real world.
thought: Doubt still comes. But I'm staying anyway.

action: He moved restlessly between the firepit and the house, tallying empty stores of food and water, worry tightening his chest each time he counted.
thought: How long can we go without eating?

action: She sensed Marcus's withdrawal and went after him, finding him at the cliff's edge and refusing to let him disappear into his own head again.
thought: I won't let him run. Not now.

action: She sat on the house porch in the shade, the baby pressing heavy against her ribs, and tried not to think about hunger or what would happen if they ran out.
thought: Will they stay when the hunger comes?

action: The pup pressed against Judy's legs on the porch, sensing her anxiety, and stayed close as the afternoon heat pressed down on the camp.
thought: She is scared. I will stay.

description: As the afternoon heat presses down and hunger starts to gnaw, Jade finds Marcus withdrawing toward the cliff again — and presses him to remember what he promised this morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're leaving us again. I can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. I'm just... thinking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About what? About how hard this is? About us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: About whether I can actually stay. When it's this hard.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can you?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't know yet. But I'm going to try.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not the same as staying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's all I have right now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then let me help. You don't have to do this alone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's the part I'm not used to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. But you're staying anyway — that's what counts.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it this morning. I still mean it now. Even when I doubt.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Good. Because we're not losing you to the cliff again.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know.

action: He sits at the fire as evening deepens, hungry and thirsty but present, his choice to stay being tested by survival.
thought: Staying is harder than I thought. But I'm here.

action: He keeps the fire burning, anxiously checking the empty water pitcher and trying to calculate how they'll feed themselves tomorrow.
thought: I should have pushed harder. I failed them.

action: She returns from the tide pools at dusk with water cupped in her hands, her calm decisive action answering the day's question.
thought: We found water. Tomorrow we fish properly.

action: She sits close to Marcus as darkness falls, the water from Jade's hands finally easing her thirst and her terror.
thought: He stayed. He meant it. I believe him.

action: The puppy presses hard against Judy's leg, trembling with hunger and fear, then slowly stills as she pulls it close.
thought: She was scared. I stayed. We're safe together.

description: As evening falls and hunger gnaws sharp, the group gathers at the fire and realizes no one foraged—Jade crosses to the tide pools for water while Marcus sits steady with Judy, proving his morning choice is real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The water's gone. So's the food. How did we let this happen?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We were here. That was the priority today.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But I'm so thirsty. And the baby—what if we can't—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We get water. We do it the way we chose this morning.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should have foraged. I should have pushed harder.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'll go to the tide pools. I know where the water collects. Twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You're leaving? You're really leaving us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm staying. You're not doing this alone.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'll keep the fire hot. Jade will be back before dark.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. I know I'm being needy. I know you said—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're eight months pregnant and scared. That's not needy. That's sense.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm going now. Stay here. I'll be fast.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're right here, Judy. We're not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Neither am I.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Found water at the tide pools. It's brackish but it's water.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Thank god. Judy, drink this slowly.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Oh. Oh, thank you. Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You keep apologizing for being human. Stop.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we fish. We take it seriously. We build a trap.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The fire stays hot. We have enough wood. We'll make it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Will you really? Even when it gets worse? Will you stay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I promised. I chose this morning. I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're all staying. We meant it and we mean it now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Even hungry. Even scared. We're doing this.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay. I believe you. I think I can sleep now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Good. We'll keep watch.

action: Sits at the fire and names his fear — wanting to leave — before choosing to stay.
thought: They heard me. I'm choosing to stay.

action: Tends the fire and listens as Marcus speaks his truth, anxiety easing into trust.
thought: He's staying. We're all staying.

action: Sits by Marcus at the fire, witnessing his vulnerability, steadying the moment as he names his choice.
thought: He doesn't have to be alone anymore.

action: Settles beside Ryan and holds the stray close, finally believing the promises won't break.
thought: They meant it. We're actually safe.

action: Pads from the darkness into the firelit circle, pressing close to Judy, finally finding safety.
thought: Safe. I'm safe with them.

description: Marcus speaks his fear at the firepit — naming the cliff, naming the hunger to leave — and chooses to stay while the group gathers close and the stray drifts into the warmth.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I keep thinking we should have been fishing all day. I let you all down.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You didn't. You were here. You stayed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But we're hungry. Really hungry now. What if—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're still together. That's what matters.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is it? When it gets worse, will you all still—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I thought about leaving. This afternoon. At the cliff.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. I went after you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I was ready to go. To stop feeling all of this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Don't say that. Please don't—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm just being honest. I was done. Then I realized something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't want to be done. Not if it means losing you.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: So you're staying? You promise?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm choosing to stay. Every day, I'll choose again.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And we're choosing you. All of us. That has to be enough.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's enough because we choose it. Together.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Even when we're starving? Even then?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Even then. That's when it matters most.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [whimper, pads over from the shadows, presses against Marcus]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: See? Everything stays. The fire, the pup, us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come here, boy. You're safe.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: We're all safe now. Because we decided we are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Rest soon. Tomorrow we try again.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Sleep soon. The fire will keep warm.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. Together. That's the plan.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And we have water now. Jade found it. That's something real.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We keep finding small things. The fire, the shelter, each other.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we'll find more. Right?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we'll find more. Judy's right.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: And you'll be here? All of you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: All of us. Always.

action: He wakes sick and finds Jade waiting at his tent—they talk about forever, then head together to the tide pools for water.
thought: Today I can stay. Tomorrow, I don't know.

action: He wakes in the house and realizes Marcus and Jade are gone—hunger gnaws at him as he waits on the porch.
thought: Are they okay? Did I mess this up?

action: She finds Marcus emerging from his tent at dawn and asks him the question she's been afraid to ask—will he stay when it gets harder?
thought: Is his answer enough? I'm not sure yet.

action: She wakes in the house and realizes how quiet it is—no voices, no footsteps—just her and the pup breathing nearby.
thought: Will they come back to me?

action: It wakes nestled against Judy's side and stretches into the morning, its ears alert but its body calm and trusting.
thought: She's here. Safe. We're still together.

description: Jade finds Marcus at dawn and asks if his promise lasts beyond today; his honest answer sends them both to the tide pools for water.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You awake? The camp is dry, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. Give me a moment.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're sick. How are you even going to help?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm going. You're not dragging water alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Last night you said you'd stay. Every day, you said. Every day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did you? Because tomorrow we're still hungry. The day after, we're still tired. We might never be full, never be safe.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So why stay? Why choose us if it's always going to be this hard?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because you're here. Because Ryan keeps building like we're staying forever. Because that pup fell asleep against Judy without flinching.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not an answer, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's the only one I have. I'm here today. Tomorrow I'll choose again. Beyond that—I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You can't promise me anything.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. But I'm standing right here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's enough. For now, that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then let's make sure today has water.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Let's go.

action: He walks with Ryan to the tide pools, gathers water, and returns to sit at the fire, steady but exhausted.
thought: I keep them safe, even like this.

action: He pushes himself to the tide pools despite the sickness, gathers water, and returns to collapse by the fire.
thought: They need me to be strong right now.

action: She follows them to the tide pools, watches the struggle, and returns to the fire with doubt blooming in her chest.
thought: Can they really stay when everything's this hard?

action: She waits at camp with the stray, watching the path and fighting against the fear that they won't come back.
thought: Will they come back? Will they stay?

action: The pup stays close to Judy, sensing her anxiety and pressing against her legs whenever she looks toward the path.
