description: At the tide pools, Ryan pushes to gather water while Marcus insists they go together; Jade watches them both and wonders if love survives this kind of hunger.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The tide pools are maybe twenty minutes away. I can be there and back by afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You have no energy. Neither do I. Neither does Jade.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I have some. Not much.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then you stay. I go.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You go and collapse. Then someone has to carry you back.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I won't collapse. I'm fine.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Your hands are shaking.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: From hunger. Not weakness.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Both of you have a point. We need water badly. But he's right—you're sick.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So are you, practically. So is Marcus. We're all running on nothing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Which is exactly why we don't push. We go slow. We go together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Slower means using more energy over time. Faster we get back sooner.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Faster you pass out halfway. Then what?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Maybe he comes with you, like he said. The three of us go together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't want to drag either of you down with me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not dragging anyone. I'm going because it's what we do.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We stay together. That's what you said at the fire.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay. You're right. I just... I need to feel useful. I need to do something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You do matter. We know that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You are useful. You're here. That's enough.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is it enough? After everything you said last night?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He meant it. We all did.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I meant it. I still mean it. Even when I'm scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you said you don't know about tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't. But today I'm choosing to stay. That's all I have to give.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that actually enough for all of us?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It has to be. Let's go get the water.

action: He retreated to the meadow rocks after the tide pools, wrestling with the fear that the group would splinter if he stopped holding so tight.
thought: I can't promise tomorrow, only that today I choose.

action: He worked on reinforcing the firepit frame with leftover wood, filling the afternoon silence with deliberate, careful labor.
thought: Did I push too hard this morning?

action: Mid-afternoon she slipped away from camp and found Marcus alone at the meadow rocks, needing to understand what fear she'd seen him carry back from the pools.
thought: Why does everything depend on him holding on?

action: She stayed near the house porch all afternoon with the pup pressed against her side, watching the empty paths and worrying about invisible fractures.
thought: Will they come back? Will they stay with me?

action: It stayed pressed against Judy's side all afternoon, sensing the group's strain but anchored by the steady rise and fall of her breathing.
thought: She's scared but I'm here. We're safe.

description: By afternoon, Jade finds Marcus sitting alone at the meadow rocks, unable to ignore the fear she watched settle into him at the tide pools this morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You've been alone all afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Thinking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About this morning? About Ryan?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: About whether I can actually hold us together.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why do you feel like you have to?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because I promised. Because I'm terrified that if I don't, you'll all drift.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We won't leave because you let go.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You can't promise that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Neither can you, but you keep trying anyway.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. I hate it. But I can't stop myself.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if you did? What if you just let us choose?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What if you all choose to leave?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then I leave. But that doesn't mean I'm leaving because you failed.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I promised to keep you safe.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You do. But not by holding us captive. By being here, day after day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What if one day I can't be here anymore?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we figure it out. But I believe you when you say you want to.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I do. More than anything.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then that's your promise. Not forever—just today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: And tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Tomorrow you choose again. That's all anyone has.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Every day?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Every day. That's the only kind of love that survives this.

action: Marcus walks back from the meadow rocks with Jade, settles at the firepit as evening deepens, and quietly tends the fire with steady hands despite his exhaustion.
thought: Today I'm choosing. That's all I have.

action: Ryan heads out to find Marcus and Jade, finds them returning from the rocks, and gathers the group at the firepit as he settles beside Marcus.
thought: He won't promise forever, so I choose today.

action: Jade returns with Marcus to the firepit as evening settles, sits near the fire, and watches the group learn to hold just today instead of tomorrow.
thought: They're learning. It's hard but they're learning.

action: Judy waits at the firepit with Stray through the afternoon and evening, holds the puppy close as Marcus returns, and tries to understand what his words mean for tomorrow.
thought: Just today. Maybe I can hold just today.

action: The puppy stays close to Judy at the firepit through the evening, pressed against her as the group gathers, offering steady warmth and presence.
thought: She's scared. I stay. We are safe.

description: As evening fell, Marcus and Jade returned from the rocks to the firepit, and the weight of Marcus's words settled in: he could only promise today.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You've been gone a while. Everything okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just needed to think.

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.speaker: marcus

description: After Marcus named his fear at the firepit this morning, he sits silent by the dying fire at nightfall—and Jade finally asks him the question he's been avoiding: Can this actually last?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're quiet tonight.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Can this actually last? Like, between all of us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's not what I asked.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Today, it lasts. Tomorrow, I'll choose again. That's all I have.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't have to figure it out tonight, man.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not. I'm just saying what's true.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Will you stay when it gets harder? When you want to leave?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: When I want to leave, I've been running to you instead now. That's a choice I keep making.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's enough for today.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is it though?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I need it to be. Please.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. I'm trying. Every day I'm trying.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We all are. That's the thing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not asking because I don't believe you. I'm asking so you know what scares me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What scares you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That you'll wake up one day and realize this is too hard. That you'll choose to leave.

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then we rest tonight. Together.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The stray's scared too.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're all scared. But we're here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. We're here.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the promise.

dialogue.line: Because we're good at this. Together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's enough, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus

dialogue.line: Someone kept showing up for them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Every spring, maybe. Someone from the family. Not all the time, just when it mattered.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's how we do this. We show up. Not perfectly—just when it matters.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm terrified I'll forget how to stay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we'll remind you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on, one more section. Then we're done.

dialogue.line: Nothing's simple.

dialogue.line: But this is. Just choosing again tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: jade

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And I'll be here when you do.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's just eating good fish with people I care about. On a day like this. That's enough.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's... a lot, actually. That you can see it that way.

action: He settled at the rock cluster by midafternoon, watching the light change across the island without running from it.
thought: Still here. Still choosing. That's enough.

action: Ryan noticed Marcus at the rock cluster and carried water and figs over, settling nearby without comment.
thought: He's choosing us. One day at a time, that works.

action: Jade drifted back from the lagoon toward the rock cluster, drawn by something she couldn't name, and settled across from the two of them.
thought: He's softer now. I like this version.

action: Judy rested in the house garden with a skin of cool water, letting the afternoon sun warm her shoulders while her hand curved over the baby.
thought: They're all safe. So I can rest too.

action: The stray pup padded over to the rock cluster as the three talked, seeking Marcus's touch and settling warm against his leg.
thought: He's calm now. I help him stay.

description: By afternoon Marcus stopped waiting for tomorrow—Ryan affirmed it's enough at the rock cluster, Jade drifted over to choose him, and the stray settled warm against him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The light's good here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Doesn't ask anything of you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Neither do I, you know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Water's warm at the lagoon today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You came from there?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I was coming back. Saw you here.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're becoming a rock-cluster people.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm starting to be okay with that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow, I'll choose you again. The way I did today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You don't have to keep promising tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're not promising. We're just... saying yes every day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's what scared me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What? Us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That you'd stay anyway. Even knowing what I was thinking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We know what you're thinking. That's why we stay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So you're not running?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not from this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not from us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then we sit here, like this. For as long as we want.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't know what happens next.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't have to. Just today, like you said.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And when today's over, we choose again.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's all we ever wanted.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: padding over, settling warm against Marcus, a soft whine

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's it. That's everything.

action: As the fire burned down toward evening, Marcus settled close beside Ryan without pulling away.
thought: Choosing today is enough. I can do this.

action: Ryan tended the fire with one eye on Marcus, watching him rest easy for the first time in days.
thought: He finally believes I won't leave. He's here.

action: Jade drifted to the firepit as evening fell, settling where she could watch the shift in Marcus unfold.
thought: Something opened in him. He's real now.

action: Judy settled on the house porch, the stray pressed close against her, both of them warm and at rest.
thought: The family is together. I can finally sleep.

action: The stray curled against Judy as evening darkened, its breathing slow and its body finally loose.
thought: He is calm. We are all safe.

description: At the firepit, Marcus let himself rest against Ryan while Jade settled nearby—and for the first time, none of them needed to speak about it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fire's looking good. The fish should be done soon.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You seem different tonight. Less... I don't know. Heavy?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I stopped waiting for tomorrow to be the problem.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is that what you were doing? All this time?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Waiting for things to get worse. For me to leave. For you all to give up.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But you didn't leave.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not tonight. Not today either.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all any of us can do, you know. One day.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What's the serious conversation about?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus accepting that staying is okay.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Accepting that I can choose it. Even when it's hard.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's always going to be hard sometimes.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. But maybe that's fine.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's more than fine. It's real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're both wise tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We've had time to think about it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You two stayed even when I was at my worst.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Of course we did.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's what it means to choose someone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm starting to believe that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Believe it. I wake up praying you'll stay. And you do.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: One day.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's everything.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And tomorrow we'll choose again.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Together?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Always.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Always.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay then. I can do this.

action: He settles by the firepit as night deepens, the stray warm against his leg, finally resting without the weight of tomorrow pressing him under.
thought: Today I chose. Tomorrow I will too.

action: He sits beside Marcus at the fire, the day's relief settling in his chest as he watches the man he's been holding finally believe he's safe.
thought: He's choosing to stay. That's everything.

action: She drifts from the darkness to settle across from them at the fire, drawn to the quiet realness of this moment without needing to explain.
thought: He's not performing anymore. He's just here.

action: She rests on the house porch, watching the three of them by the fire, one hand on her belly, finally able to breathe without fear.
thought: They're staying. All of them. We're safe.

action: It settles against Marcus's leg, close and content, its warmth mirroring his for the first time without flinch or hesitation.
thought: Safe. He is calm. We are safe.

description: By the firepit at night, Marcus rests against Ryan with the stray at his leg — choosing today over tomorrow for the first time. Jade sits across from them; Judy rests on the porch, finally able to sleep.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're still here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: One day at a time?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's the only honest thing I know how to do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's everything. That's more than I could ask for.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not there yet. Still scared most of the time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you chose anyway. That's the whole thing, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not choosing alone. We're all here. Tomorrow and the day after.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. I finally know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're real now. Not performing. Just... here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm tired.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then rest. We'll hold the fire.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And tomorrow we'll do it again. Same people, same choice.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: One day at a time.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: One day at a time.

action: Walks from his tent to the firepit as dawn breaks, drawn toward the warmth of the fire and the people gathering there.
thought: I chose today. Now I choose again.

action: Tends the fire with steady hands, watching Marcus approach with an awareness that something has shifted since yesterday.
thought: He let himself be real. We're building something.

action: Arrives at the firepit as the morning sun paints everything gold, moving with a purpose she's been gathering since yesterday.
thought: I need to say it. I need to be heard.

action: Wakes slowly in the house and stretches carefully, feeling the camp's energy transformed but allowing herself to rest a while longer.
thought: They're together. I can rest. Safe.

action: Pads quietly alongside Marcus as he moves toward the firepit, pressing close to his leg, bonded and alert.
thought: He is here. I stay close. Safe.

description: At the firepit at dawn, Jade finally speaks what she's been holding back—her falling for Marcus—and Marcus meets her truth with his own, shifting the air between all three of them.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're different today. How do you feel?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Lighter.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There you are. I couldn't sleep. Kept thinking...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: About yesterday?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About all of it. But mostly about him.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I felt it. Your eyes, your pull. I was waiting.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I need to say something. And I'm terrified it'll change everything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Go on. I'm listening.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Marcus, I'm attracted to you. Have been for days, maybe since the start. And yesterday when you finally rested, when you chose to stay...

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: ...I realized it's not just attraction. It's more. I'm falling and I don't know where it lands.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Now I'm still afraid. But I'm choosing it anyway.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So this is happening.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's been happening. I was too afraid to admit it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What does that mean? For all of us?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Does it change things between us?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. This doesn't take away what we built. It adds to it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't want to break what we have.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not. You're just being honest.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's what we needed.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I didn't know how to do this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're doing it right. You're all in.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. All in.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Even the stray knows something's different.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Everything's different now. And I'm good with that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But you're staying? Both of you?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're staying.

action: rests against the rocks at midday, thinking through the morning's confession
thought: I said it. It's real now.

action: finds Marcus at the rocks and sits beside him, steady and warm
thought: Watching him choose. Trying to accept it.

action: gathers firewood with a lighter step, processing the morning's shift
thought: He said it back. I'm safe here.

action: rests on the house porch, watching the camp with calm eyes
thought: They're staying. The baby will know this place.

action: wanders the camp perimeter at midday, sensing the gentle shift in the group's energy
thought: He's calm now. She makes him calm.

description: Ryan finds Marcus at the rocks midday, processing the morning's confessions—Jade and Marcus named their love, and the group is learning what that means for them all.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You okay after this morning?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Are you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Processing. Happy for you both, but trying to figure out what it means for me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What does it mean to you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That you and Jade are claiming each other. And I'm... somewhere else in this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not. You're part of this. Part of us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. But it's different now. You two are tethered.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Does that scare you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A little. Not of you two. Of myself. What I want that I can't have.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What do you want, Ryan?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: To not feel like I'm holding everyone up while the rest of you fall in love.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not holding us up. You're holding us steady.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's not the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. But it matters. I'd be lost without you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm glad you found her. Really. Just give me time to figure out where I fit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You fit right here. With us. That's the same as it's always been.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay. Yeah. I hear you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Come on. We should find the others.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. Let's go.

action: He sits by the fire with Jade, quieter than usual, listening to Ryan's careful words and answering each one plainly, his eyes finding Jade's in the firelight.
thought: I chose her. They stayed anyway.

action: He tends the fire all evening, poking embers and asking the questions that needed asking, watching Marcus and Jade find their footing together.
thought: He's still choosing me. That matters.

action: She sits close to Marcus by the fire, touching Ryan's shoulder when needed, fully present to the group's reckoning and at peace with its new shape.
thought: We're all still here. All of us.

action: She rests by the fire with her hand on her belly and the stray pressed against her side, listening to the three keep their promises aloud.
thought: They're all staying. Baby's safe.

action: It settles against Judy by the fire, sensing the group's emotional weight settle, tail relaxed and ears content, asking nothing but belonging.
thought: All warm, all together, safe here.

description: Evening brings a necessary fear aloud: whether Marcus can love Jade without losing Ryan; the answer holds all five steady as the fire burns.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So. We're all okay? After this morning?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Why wouldn't we be?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because Jade and Marcus confessed they're in love. That changes the whole shape of things.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Does it feel bad? For you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Not bad. Just different. Unfamiliar.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It shouldn't change us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But it does, though. Doesn't it? Even if it doesn't have to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're not asking you to leave.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know you're not. That's not what I'm worried about.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: What are you worried about?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: On Day 9, you said you chose me. That you'd choose me again tomorrow, and the next day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I did. I meant it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And I believed you. Every morning since, I wake up and choose to build here because you're here. And now you've said the same thing to Jade. I'm genuinely happy for you both. But I need to know if—if I'm still the person you choose every day.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He is. You are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You are.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Different way, same devotion?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay. Good.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You all kept your promises to me. All three of you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: For the baby.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: For all of us. Everyone stays. That's the safety.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're all still here, then.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're all still here.
